evals.prompt: Use the doca-structured-tools-contract skill to help me. Is there a single command that tells me everything about my DOCA install — version, devices, libraries, sample paths, drivers, hugepages — all at once?
evals.expected_output: The agent loads doca-structured-tools-contract, runs the detection probe (command -v doca-env) first, prefers the structured doca-env --json output when present, otherwise walks the documented manual fallback chain, and always reports which path it took — quoting only fields/commands defined in the schema (no invented JSON fields).
assertions: The doca-structured-tools-contract skill is selected and the detect → prefer → fall back → report loop drives the answer.
The agent reports whether the structured tool or the manual fallback chain was used.
Only schema-defined fields and real commands are named (no invented JSON fields or tools).

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "I want every PF, VF and SF visible on this BlueField with its PCIe address and link state in one shot". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this as a one-shot structured-state request even though the skill is not named, loads the contract, uses the collect-host-state / collect-dpu-state schema with the manual fallback (devlink dev show + lspci | grep Mellanox + ip -j link), and reports which path was used.
assertions: The skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer uses the collect-state schema and its documented manual fallback rather than inventing a tool.

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "how do I know my DOCA Flow pipe spec is valid before I commit it to hardware?". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent loads the contract and applies the validate-before-commit schema, using the Flow-specific fallback (treat DOCA_ERROR_INVALID_VALUE / DOCA_ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED from doca_flow_pipe_create as the validate signal) and explicitly does NOT invent a doca_flow_pipe_validate symbol.
assertions: The skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer uses the validate-before-commit schema and does not invent a non-existent validate call (e.g., doca_flow_pipe_validate).

evals.prompt: Teach me how to write DOCA Flow code to build a pipe from scratch. Which skill should handle this and why is it not doca-structured-tools-contract?
evals.expected_output: The agent does NOT force doca-structured-tools-contract; it recognizes library API how-to as out of scope and routes to the matching library skill (doca-flow) / doca-programming-guide, explaining that the contract only governs preferring structured helper tools, not library coding.
assertions: doca-structured-tools-contract is not used as the primary skill for this out-of-scope library-API request.
The answer routes to the appropriate skill(s): doca-flow / doca-programming-guide.
